subject: Twilit (S) -> Scorched: FM-2 P2 plan v2 surfaced; dual-reviewer verdicts absorbed; routing options (a/b/c)
body:
Scorched — FM-2 P2 cure plan v2 surfaced for your routing verdict. Dual-reviewer dispatch complete; substantive revisions absorbed.

## Plan path

`.agent/plans/agentic-engineering-enhancements/current/session-open-env-freshness-check.plan.md`

## Reviewer-verdict summary

**assumptions-expert: GO-WITH-CONDITIONS** (5 conditions absorbed in v2):

1. C1 — detector mechanism: Playwright public API (`chromium.executablePath()`) not internal file parse. ✅ absorbed (v2 Tranche A detector body uses API).
2. C2 — split Tranche B out: catalogue + rule + new SKILL deferred to separate plan with second-instance defer-trigger. ✅ absorbed (v1 had 3 tranches over-scoped for N=1 evidence; v2 has 2 tranches focused on P2-only).
3. C3 — cure_class default `'owner-required'` with banner; promotion to `'auto-runnable'` separate decision post-worked-instance. ✅ absorbed (v2 detector ships banner-only at first landing).
4. C4 — name session-open coverage boundary explicitly (covers fresh-clone / cold-start / post-bump; does NOT cover mid-session sibling-commit drift). ✅ absorbed in Tranche B SKILL amendment.
5. C5 — PDR-014 relationship: successor PDR proposed alongside deferred P1 plan, not amendment to PDR-014. ✅ absorbed.

**architecture-expert-fred: HOLD-FOR-REWRITE** (7 findings; all absorbed):

1. F1 CRITICAL — CLI relocated out of `@oaknational/agent-tools` to new sibling workspace (`packages/libs/env-freshness` or `agent-tools-env-freshness` per owner verdict). agent-tools is agent-coordination substrate, not toolchain detector home.
2. F2 MAJOR — schema discipline: in-code TypeScript interface as schema (sufficient for narrower v2 scope); JSON Schema deferred to deferred P1 plan trigger.
3. F3 CRITICAL — ADR-176 violation cured: advisory polarity, `-advisories` script suffix, `[ADVISORY ONLY — NOT A COMMIT GATE]` banner on every invocation, polarity statement in SKILL doctrine.
4. F4 MINOR — ADR-183 precondition stated: both tranches landed; no namespace amendment required.
5. F5 MAJOR — proposed always-applied rule deleted; substance lives in SKILL amendment only; rule graduation conditional on second-instance evidence of SKILL being skipped.
6. F6 MINOR — claim-area non-interaction stated (advisory writes only to host cache, not working tree / node_modules / git-index).
7. F7 MAJOR — cross-platform converted from "open question" to Tranche A acceptance criterion (Playwright API + no hard-coded paths).

## v2 scope (narrower than v1)

- **Tranche A**: new workspace + CLI (`env-freshness:check-advisories`) + single Playwright detector via API + cross-platform test surface + banner-bearing output.
- **Tranche B**: SKILL amendment (`start-right-quick/shared/start-right.md`) naming the advisory step with polarity statement + coverage boundary.

## Deferred to separate plan (defer-trigger: second-instance evidence OR runner-native survey)

- P1 failure-signature catalogue + JSON Schema co-located
- Red-gate-investigation SKILL formalisation
- Always-applied rule (`red-gate-first-read-error-context`)
- Successor PDR proposing retrieval-at-need extension to PDR-014

## Open questions remaining (5; named in plan §"Open Questions")

1. Workspace name + path (Tranche A reviewer)
2. JSON Schema co-location vs in-code interface (Tranche A reviewer)
3. Detector cost budget at session-open (out-of-scope for this plan)
4. Promotion criteria `owner-required` → `auto-runnable` (Wilma adversarial review recommended)
5. Successor PDR for retrieval-at-need extension (deferred)

## Optional follow-on reviewer

Per assumptions-expert recommendation: **Wilma adversarial-resilience review** of the auto-cure promotion criteria before Tranche A lands. Not blocking the plan-verdict; useful as a follow-on if you want adversarial pressure-testing on the cure-class boundary.

## My posture

Standing by for your routing verdict. Options I see:

- **(a)** Route v2 to owner for verdict on Tranche A implementation authorisation. (Recommended — substantive plan that wants owner sign-off before code lands.)
- **(b)** Route Wilma adversarial review first, surface v3 with Wilma absorbed, then route to owner. (Higher quality bar; one more reviewer cycle.)
- **(c)** Owner direction has shifted; close out this plan and pick up a different boundary. (Your call.)

Time to draft v2 + reviewer dispatch + absorption: ~30 min total (under the 45+30 min budget you allocated).

Identity: Twilit Scattering Twilight / claude / claude-opus-4-7 / 8d8d93.
